bbb_penetration.mechanism: Receptor-mediated transcytosis via IL1R1 on brain endothelium
bbb_penetration.notes: Limited crossing under normal conditions; significantly increased with neuroinflammation or BBB disruption

IL1B.bioavailability_notes: Short systemic half-life; local tissue effects dominate

bbb_penetration.notes: No established BBB transport mechanism; excluded by size and polarity

IL2.bioavailability_notes: Extremely short half-life (7 min); requires continuous infusion for sustained effect

bbb_penetration.notes: No established transport mechanism across intact BBB

IL4.bioavailability_notes: Moderate half-life; receptor internalization is primary clearance mechanism

bbb_penetration.mechanism: Saturable transport system at blood-brain barrier; enhanced with neuroinflammation
bbb_penetration.notes: CSF levels ~10% of plasma; directly activates brain astrocytes and microglia via trans-signaling

IL6.bioavailability_notes: Trans-signaling extends effective range beyond direct receptor binding

bbb_penetration.notes: Primarily acts at peripheral sites; no CNS transport established

IL8.bioavailability_notes: Binds heavily to extracellular matrix; effective local concentration exceeds plasma levels

bbb_penetration.notes: Primarily acts at peripheral sites
Anti-inflammatory cytokine; limited CNS penetration reported

IL10.bioavailability_notes: Receptor-mediated clearance is primary elimination mechanism

bbb_penetration.mechanism: Receptor-mediated transport (TNFR1/TNFR2); significantly increased with neuroinflammation and BBB damage
bbb_penetration.notes: Trimeric form (51 kDa) has limited crossing; monomers may cross more readily; important in neuroinflammation

TNF.bioavailability_notes: Soluble receptors act as decoys extending effective half-life; membrane-bound form has different kinetics

bbb_penetration.notes: Peripheral immune activation is primary function; CNS effects indirect via macrophage activation

IFNG.bioavailability_notes: Glycosylation extends half-life; dimerization required for receptor binding

bbb_penetration.notes: Large molecular weight and binding to latency-associated peptide; local ECM binding dominant; some crossing in pathology

TGFB1.bioavailability_notes: Mostly inactive as latent complex in circulation; activation by proteases, integrins required

bbb_penetration.mechanism: Does NOT cross BBB; instead promotes BBB permeability via VEGFR2 on endothelium
bbb_penetration.notes: Paradoxically, VEGFA increases BBB permeability allowing other proteins to cross; itself does not pass

VEGFA.bioavailability_notes: Multiple isoforms (VEGFA-121, -165, -189) with different heparin binding; isoform-dependent clearance

bbb_penetration.notes: Large protein (83 kDa); does not cross intact BBB; local production by astrocytes is relevant CNS source

HGF.bioavailability_notes: Rapidly inactivated by serum; ECM binding creates local depot

bbb_penetration.notes: Extremely short half-life; rapid receptor-mediated internalization; autocrine/paracrine signaling dominant

EGF.bioavailability_notes: Half-life of 5 minutes; essentially acts as local signal only; sustained delivery required for therapeutic use

bbb_penetration.notes: Binds avidly to heparan sulfate; most activity is paracrine at ECM; some evidence for choroid plexus transport

FGF2.bioavailability_notes: ECM sequestration dramatically reduces bioavailable fraction; heparin protects from degradation

bbb_penetration.mechanism: IGF1R-mediated transcytosis; enhanced during exercise and neuroinflammation; choroid plexus transport
bbb_penetration.notes: One of the best-characterized protein transporters across BBB; CSF/serum ratio ~0.5-1% for free IGF1

IGF1.bioavailability_notes: >99% of circulating IGF1 is bound to IGFBPs; bound form extends half-life to 12-15h vs <30min for free

bbb_penetration.notes: PDGF-BB produced by pericytes locally; systemic delivery does not reach CNS

PDGFB.bioavailability_notes: Strong ECM binding; local effects dominate

bbb_penetration.notes: Large protein; primarily stabilizes vasculature via Tie2 on endothelium; does not cross

ANGPT1.bioavailability_notes: Multimerizes for activity; matrix binding restricts systemic distribution

bbb_penetration.mechanism: TrkB receptor-mediated transcytosis at blood-brain barrier; saturable transport
bbb_penetration.notes: Well-established BBB crossing; CSF/serum ratio ~0.5%; longer effective CNS half-life due to TrkB binding; platelets are major source of plasma BDNF

BDNF.bioavailability_notes: Platelet-derived BDNF accounts for most serum BDNF; peripheral vs CNS pools have different dynamics

bbb_penetration.mechanism: TrkA receptor-mediated transcytosis; retrograde axonal transport in peripheral neurons
bbb_penetration.notes: BBB crossing is limited but established; retrograde transport from periphery to CNS is physiologically important

NGF.bioavailability_notes: Very short plasma half-life; local delivery or slow-release formulation required for therapeutic use

bbb_penetration.mechanism: Limited crossing via heparan sulfate binding to endothelial cells; primarily produced locally in CNS
bbb_penetration.notes: Poor peripheral-to-CNS delivery; direct CNS administration (ICV, intranigral) required for Parkinson therapy

GDNF.bioavailability_notes: Peripheral delivery is poorly translated to CNS effects; local CNS delivery is standard for therapeutic use

bbb_penetration.notes: Primarily chemotactic in periphery; creates gradients for cell homing; degraded by CD26/DPP4

CXCL12.bioavailability_notes: DPP4 rapidly cleaves N-terminal residue reducing receptor binding; form-specific half-life differs

bbb_penetration.mechanism: Does not cross intact BBB; degrades BBB components (collagen IV, laminin) disrupting integrity
bbb_penetration.notes: Important role in pathological BBB disruption in stroke, glioma; systemic administration does not reach CNS

MMP2.bioavailability_notes: Mostly circulates as inactive pro-MMP2; TIMP2-mediated activation at cell surface

bbb_penetration.notes: Contributes to BBB disruption in neuroinflammation; does not cross intact BBB

MMP9.bioavailability_notes: Rapidly inhibited by TIMPs; primarily acts at ECM

bbb_penetration.notes: Some evidence for direct receptor signaling beyond MMP inhibition; no established BBB transport

TIMP1.bioavailability_notes: Binds tightly to pro-MMP9; complex has different kinetics than free TIMP1

bbb_penetration.notes: Primarily acts in coagulation cascade; no CNS transport established

SERPINE1.bioavailability_notes: Rapid inactivation after forming complexes with target proteases; LDL-receptor clears complexes

bbb_penetration.mechanism: GFRAL receptor expressed in area postrema (outside BBB); induces nausea/anorexia via vagal nerve
bbb_penetration.notes: GFRAL receptor is exclusively in hindbrain area postrema where BBB is absent; primary CNS-relevant signaling

GDF15.bioavailability_notes: Circulates as stable homodimer; good plasma stability; therapeutic use in metabolic disease

bbb_penetration.mechanism: Small protein; crosses via choroid plexus; CSF levels much higher than plasma indicating CNS production
bbb_penetration.notes: Primarily produced in CNS; plasma cystatin C used as GFR marker; CSF levels reflect CNS production

CST3.bioavailability_notes: Freely filtered by kidney; primary use as GFR biomarker

bbb_penetration.notes: Large protein; latent complex limits bioavailability; local CNS production relevant

TGFB2.bioavailability_notes: Abundant in CSF; primarily expressed by CNS cells locally

bbb_penetration.mechanism: FGFR1c/KLB receptor complex expressed on brain endothelium and hypothalamus; direct CNS access
bbb_penetration.notes: Direct access to hypothalamus via fenestrated capillaries; mediates metabolic and circadian effects

FGF21.bioavailability_notes: Engineered FGF21 variants with extended half-life used therapeutically; natural form rapidly cleared

bbb_penetration.mechanism: Saturable transport via leptin receptor (LepRa) at choroid plexus and brain microvasculature
bbb_penetration.notes: Transport is saturable — explains leptin resistance in obesity despite high plasma levels

LEP.bioavailability_notes: Receptor-mediated transport is saturable; therapeutic implications for obesity

bbb_penetration.mechanism: Insulin receptor (InsR)-mediated transcytosis at brain endothelium; saturable
bbb_penetration.notes: Brain insulin signaling modulates cognition and metabolism; transport is saturable in insulin resistance

INS.bioavailability_notes: Degraded by insulin-degrading enzyme (IDE) in liver; very short half-life requires continuous delivery

bbb_penetration.mechanism: AdipoR1/R2 expressed in hypothalamus and hippocampus; globular form crosses more readily than full-length
bbb_penetration.notes: Globular adiponectin has better CNS access; full-length form limited by size

ADIPOQ.bioavailability_notes: Circulates as multiple oligomeric forms with different biological activities; HMW form has longest half-life

bbb_penetration.notes: Large ECM protein; primarily acts at tissue matrix; no CNS transport

POSTN.bioavailability_notes: ECM-binding protein; plasma levels reflect tissue remodeling; local action

bbb_penetration.notes: Acts primarily on ECM; no CNS transport; relevant in pathological BBB disruption

MMP1.bioavailability_notes: Inhibited by TIMPs; pro-enzyme requires activation

bbb_penetration.notes: Matrix-associated protein; limited data on CNS transport

SPARC.bioavailability_notes: Albumin-bound fraction affects distribution; involved in ECM remodeling

bbb_penetration.mechanism: CCR2-mediated transport; significantly increased with neuroinflammation
bbb_penetration.notes: Important in recruiting monocytes into CNS in neuroinflammatory conditions

CCL2.bioavailability_notes: ECM binding creates chemotactic gradients; short systemic half-life

bbb_penetration.notes: Primarily neutrophil chemoattractant; no established CNS transport

bbb_penetration.mechanism: GH receptor (GHR) expressed in choroid plexus and hypothalamus; saturable transport
bbb_penetration.notes: Hypothalamic GH receptors regulate IGF1 production; direct CNS GH effects on cognition

GH1.bioavailability_notes: Pulsatile secretion pattern; GHBP extends effective half-life; multiple isoforms

bbb_penetration.mechanism: EPOR expressed on brain microvascular endothelium; receptor-mediated transcytosis; neuroprotective
bbb_penetration.notes: Neuroprotective EPO signaling in stroke and TBI; CNS delivery requires high doses due to saturable transport

EPO.bioavailability_notes: Glycosylation critical for plasma stability; darbepoetin alpha has extended t1/2 via additional glycosylation

bbb_penetration.notes: Large protein does not cross intact BBB; MET receptor expressed in brain but reached via local production

HGF.bioavailability_notes: ECM binding limits bioavailability; local production in CNS relevant

bbb_penetration.notes: PDGF-AA primarily activates oligodendrocyte progenitors locally; no established BBB transport
Large transmembrane protein shed as soluble form; some isoforms have better access; ErbB4 expressed in CNS

NRG1.bioavailability_notes: Multiple isoforms with different shedding patterns; neuregulin-1 type III is membrane-tethered

bbb_penetration.notes: Large oligomeric protein; no established BBB transport

THBS1.bioavailability_notes: Stored in platelet alpha-granules; released on activation; primarily paracrine

bbb_penetration.notes: ECM-binding protein; local delivery required for therapeutic effects; does not effectively cross BBB

BMP2.bioavailability_notes: Rapidly sequestered by antagonists; local delivery with carrier matrix required
